member.name: Dr Jeevun Sandher
member.synopsis: Dr Jeevun Sandher is the Labour MP for Loughborough , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to help improve local bus services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is determined to provide better bus services for communities across the country. Our Bus Services Act will empower local authorities to improve services, and its backed by over £3 billion in funding to support local leaders and bus operato...

writtenQuestions.heading: Fuel Cells
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that home-grown hydrogen fuel cell developers are supported by the (a) Modern Industrial Strategy and (b) Clean Energy Industries sector plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the Modern Industrial Strategy, the Government’s Clean Energy Industries Sector Plan re-iterates our commitment to driving long-term, sustainable growth in hydrogen. It provides deployment certainty with clear timelines for future deployment an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Fuel Cells
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he plans to take to support the home-grown hydrogen fuel cell sector in the proposed Hydrogen strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Industrial Strategy, through the 10 Year Clean Energy Industries Sector Plan, already backs hydrogen as a frontier technology. It clarifies how Government will capitalise on the enormous growth and jobs potential. The associated Action Plan gives the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Land Use
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, when his Department plans to implement the proposed land use framework.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government ran a consultation on land use in England from January to April this year. The responses to this consultation, and feedback from supporting workshops, are being analysed. These responses and outputs will inform the preparation of the Land U...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Coastal Erosion and Flood Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how much of the flood risk and coastal erosion risk management budget has been allocated to nature based solutions in the current financial year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Many projects in the Flood and Coastal Erosion Risk Capital Investment Programme deliver a combination of nature-based solutions (NBS) and hard defences, and we do not explicitly capture the expenditure on NBS separately. Due to this, we are unable to sep...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Coastal Erosion and Flood Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how much and what proportion of the Flood Risk and Coastal Erosion Risk Management budget was spent on nature based solutions in the (a) 2022-23, (b) 2023-24 and (c) 2024-25 financial
writtenQuestions.answerText: Many projects in the Flood and Coastal Erosion Risk Capital Investment Programme deliver a combination of nature-based solutions (NBS) and hard defences, and we do not explicitly capture the expenditure on NBS separately. Due to this, we are unable to sep...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Flood Control and Sewers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps he is taking to support land owners to implement nature based solutions to (a) drainage and (b) flood management.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Farmers and landowners have an increasingly important role to play in reducing the risk of flooding and coastal erosion, through measures such as nature-based solutions (NbS). To support this the Government is investing in NbS measures, such as natural fl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Sewers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether he plans to bring into force the legislative provisions of schedule 3 of the Flood and Water Management Act 2010 for the adoption of sustainable draining systems in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to requiring standardised Sustainable Drainage Systems (SuDS) in new developments. These should be to designs that cope with changing climatic conditions as well as delivering wider water infrastructure benefits, reduce run of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Mesothelioma: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has made an assessment of the potential merits of undertaking a National Mesothelioma Clinical Audit to (a) identify gaps in mesothelioma care, (b) optimise allocation of res
writtenQuestions.answerText: Reducing unwarranted variation in cancer care and treatment is a priority for the Government. In December 2024, my Rt Hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care committed to developing a National Cancer Plan. The National Cancer Plan w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: Apprenticeships
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to increase the number of construction apprenticeships.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government has an ambitious plan for re-building Britain, delivering 1.5 million homes in England in this Parliament. Around 5,000 more construction apprenticeship places will be made available per year by the 2027/28 financial year thanks to an £140...

writtenQuestions.heading: Flood Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether he plans to expand the Flood Resilience Scheme to households which (a) have been affected outside named storms and (b) are at risk of flooding in the future.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To ensure we protect the country from the devastating impacts of flooding, we will invest £2.4 billion in 2024/25 and 2025/26 to improve flood resilience, by building, maintaining, and repairing flood defences. Included in this programme, the Environment ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Flood Control: Leicestershire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether the Flood Recovery Framework will be activated for the flooding in Leicestershire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My thoughts remain with householders and business owners impacted by flooding after the recent heavy rainfall, flooding is a devastating experience for all those affected.Government support in the aftermath of flooding is only provided in exceptional circ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Leicestershire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve maternity services in Leicestershire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following Care Quality Commission inspections in January 2024, the trust has strong improvement plans in place for maternity services at the Leicester Royal Infirmary and Leicester General Hospital (LGH). Many actions from both the 2023 and 2024 inspectio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Ukraine: Military Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps he is taking to support Ukraine.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In chairing the UN Security Council on 18 November, the Foreign Secretary made clear that UK support for Ukraine is ironclad. We have stepped up the delivery of vital military equipment, led the way in investing in Ukraine’s defence industrial base, and i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Lampard Inquiry
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department plans to integrate the recommendations of the Lampard inquiry into mental health services nationwide.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Lampard Statutory Inquiry began public hearings in September 2024. The Department will await the findings of the Inquiry and will consider its recommendations once submitted by the Chair. In the meantime, we are working with NHS England to understand ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Loughborough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to recruit more GPs in Loughborough constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have committed to training thousands more general practitioners (GPs) across the country, as well as taking pressure off those currently working in the system. NHS England has allocated £1.9 million of emergency short term funding, from October 2024 to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Flood Control: Loughborough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps the Flood Resilience Task Force is taking to help protect communities in Loughborough constituency from flood damage.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To ensure that communities are better protected from flooding, Defra has established a ministerially led cross-Governmental Flood Resilience Taskforce, with the first meeting taking place in September. The Flood Resilience Taskforce will ensure that prepa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to support (a) clean hydrogen generation, (b) the use of hydrogen in (i) heavy industry and (ii) transportation and (c) the (A) skills and (B) infrastructure required
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is delivering revenue support to hydrogen production through the Hydrogen Production Business Model. This is being combined with demand-side measures including innovation and capital funding for industrial and transport users, as well as th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Loughborough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how investors in hydrogen projects in Loughborough constituency can request funding from (a) the National Wealth Fund and (b) GB Energy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Wealth Fund was announced by the Chancellor on 9 July and further details will be set out at the International Investment Summit on 14 October. In the meantime, projects can approach UKIB who assesses each opportunity on a deal-by-deal basis ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
